title: Make sidebar more minimal
description: Update the app sidebar toward the provided minimal reference: compact dark workspace list, subdued chrome, status dots, badges, and active row treatment.
notes: Implemented scoped sidebar visual updates and added per-project custom emoji icon support via Project Settings. Icons are preserved with automatic fallback when no custom emoji is set.
close_reason: Completed minimal sidebar styling and per-project custom emoji icons; npm run build passes. Requested external CMake build could not run because /home/user/Programs/Pure Harmony Midi Editor/plugin/build does not exist.

title: Research open-source opencode TUI support patterns
description: Compare open-source terminal/AI-code desktop or web apps that support opencode or similar full-screen TUIs, focusing on mouse wheel, xterm.js, PTY, and alternate-screen behavior.
notes: Research summary: Compared OpenCodeUI, 247 Claude Code Remote, cdesktop/Vibe Kanban, wmux, official opencode app, Tether, and Termly. Strongest direct pattern is 247: in alternate buffer/fullscreen TUI mode it sends SGR mouse wheel reports (CSI <64/65;x;yM) to PTY and enables tmux mouse mode. Most xterm peers only forward terminal.onData and rely on xterm local scrollback; none inspected wired terminal.onBinary, despite xterm docs saying some mouse reports use onBinary. OpenCode itself enables TUI mouse capture by default and exposes tui.json scroll_speed/scroll_acceleration/mouse options. Recommended implementation: add terminal.onBinary forwarding, move Ctrl+wheel zoom into attachCustomWheelEventHandler so normal wheel remains xterm-owned, add opencode alternate-buffer fallback to synthesize SGR wheel reports only when xterm/opencode mouse path does not handle it, and consider opencode serve/API integration as longer-term alternative.
close_reason: Research completed; findings recorded and implementation follow-up filed as Claude-Terminal-lge.

title: Implement OpenCode TUI mouse wheel forwarding
description: Implement the follow-up from Claude-Terminal-izw research: make OpenCode TUI wheel input work in the embedded xterm terminal. Scope: forward xterm onBinary to PTY, move Ctrl+wheel zoom into attachCustomWheelEventHandler so normal wheel events are not blocked, preserve normal-buffer xterm scrollback, and add an OpenCode alternate-buffer fallback that sends SGR wheel reports (CSI <64/65;col;rowM) only when needed. Verify with OpenCode TUI and a mouse-wheel/fullscreen TUI scenario.
acceptance_criteria: 1. xterm onBinary data is forwarded to the PTY path without breaking existing onData input buffering. 2. Ctrl+wheel still changes terminal font size and normal wheel events continue through xterm. 3. OpenCode alternate-screen wheel input scrolls/acts inside the TUI. 4. Normal scrollback wheel behavior still works for non-TUI output. 5. Focused tests or manual verification notes cover the new behavior.
notes: Implemented: xterm custom wheel handler now owns wheel handling, Ctrl+wheel still zooms and returns false to xterm, normal wheel returns true so xterm can process scroll/mouse behavior, onBinary now forwards mouse-report binary data to the PTY, and OpenCode alternate-buffer fallback synthesizes SGR wheel reports only when mouse tracking is not active. Added focused unit tests for Ctrl+wheel, normal scroll passthrough, and fallback SGR output. Verification: npm test -- --run src/renderer/components/terminal/useTerminalSetup/eventHandlers.test.ts passed; npm run build passed; full npm test still has unrelated pre-existing failures in session-store fs mock, pty-manager stale expectations, and theme name expectations.
close_reason: Implemented and verified with focused tests plus production build.

title: Implement Grok Build harness support
description: Add Grok Build as a supported terminal harness/backend, including launcher support for grok/agent commands, permission/resume args, CLI checks, instruction file mapping, backend selectors, and HTTP/mobile type plumbing.

title: Research opencode TUI scrollwheel support improvements
description: Investigate how Claude Terminal can improve opencode TUI mouse/scrollwheel support, including xterm.js behavior, mouse tracking, and implementation options.
notes: Research findings: opencode runs as a full-screen/mouse-reporting TUI, so scrollwheel support should be delivered as terminal mouse wheel escape sequences, not browser scrollback. Current renderer attaches a DOM wheel listener only for zoom + userScrolledUp tracking and does not use xterm attachCustomWheelEventHandler or onBinary. xterm.js supports mouse tracking modes and SGR mouse mode; its onBinary event is specifically for binary mouse reports that must be passed to the PTY as binary data. Recommended direction: let xterm process normal wheel events, use attachCustomWheelEventHandler only to intercept Ctrl+wheel zoom, wire terminal.onBinary to pty write path, and add a backend-aware fallback for opencode/alternate-screen when mouseTrackingMode is none by sending repeated ArrowUp/ArrowDown or SGR wheel events. Need validation with opencode TUI.
close_reason: Research completed; recommendations captured in task notes and final handoff.
